title: The Game
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|Outdated lore, poor execution|date=June 15, 2026}} {{Background|file=The Arcade.webp}} {{class|no=188p3=3|p1=3|p2=3|p3=2}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:The Game}}
==
Welcome to the Game of Life or Death!
== ===
Will you win, or will it be game over?'''
===
==Description== {{Blurimage|align=right|caption=A poster advertising '''The Game'''.|width=275px|file=Flyer.png}} '''The Game''' is one large level composed of a series of sub-levels arranged into a "game" for the wanderer to partake in, with the prize at the end seeming to be a safe haven. Although relatively well-hidden, it is primarily accessed from areas where a certain poster is attached, advertising The Game. The sub-levels are as follows: ==Press Start== '''“Press Start”''' is the first area of The Game that wanderers will find themselves in, serving as the point of entry into the rest of the level. It is a dark room with a perimeter of approximately 400 square feet, lacking doors and windows. The entirety of the room is painted a dark shade of purple, with a black light and glow-in-the-dark shapes tacked on the walls as the only source of light. Within the center of the room is an arcade machine, with the words "Press Start!" written on the top of the cabinet. The machine’s exact appearance varies between those who enter the level, with different buttons, joysticks, and other controls. Upon approaching the arcade machine, it will automatically boot up, emitting a sound resembling a desktop iMac start-up. Approximately one minute from the beginning of the machine's boot sequence, it will have finished loading. The screen will display information to the wanderer akin to a Terms of Service agreement, with the option to accept or deny. The information reads as follows:
===
Welcome to The Game.
=== I may not know exactly why you are here, but I can think of a few reasons. Perhaps you have been screwed over in some way, and you are looking for a second chance to get your life together. It may be that you have come to seek fortune unlike what has ever been witnessed by humankind before. Or perhaps you may just be participating for the thrill, and you love a good challenge whenever you find one. What matters is that you have found one of the flyers I have scattered about the Backrooms, and have taken up my offer. Ahead of you is a grueling game that will test your skill, wit, and every last thing you have ever learned. Do not take the upcoming challenge lightly or play foul, as those who do will face harsh consequences. Only the most worthy will make their way to the end of this game and obtain the prize I have chosen to offer. For those who are ready to play, good luck, you're going to need it.
Selecting Deny will transport the wanderer to the previous level they were in. Selecting Accept will transport the wanderer to The Stage, equipped with a switch to "pause" the game. ==The Stage== '''“The Stage”''' is a large maze, the layout of which is always structured uniquely for the wanderer(s) within it. This maze consists of wooden carpeted hallways, similar to those in [[Level 27]], with arcade machines scattered throughout. This part of the level serves as the actual "game" that the wanderer must partake in. Various [[Object List|items]] are strewn about the maze, which wanderers may use to their advantage, especially to defend themselves from the hostile entities they will encounter. At the center of the maze is a unique entity known as '''The Boss''', which the wanderer must kill in order to reach the exit. Within this area, wanderers are given "three lives" – meaning they are allowed to die twice, but if they die for a third time, they will be transported to Game Over. If needed, they may temporarily transport themselves to Game Paused for a brief break before returning to the game. Attempts to exploit or tamper with the game in any way will banish the wanderer to You Cheated. However, if the wanderer successfully kills The Boss and reaches the exit, they will be transported to You Win, having beaten the game. =='''Game Paused'''==
This game is meant to be a grueling gauntlet that only the strongest survive, that much is no secret if you read the article or are currently a player. The amount of mercy I offer is very limited seeing as the one who truly wins this game will be rewarded with both immense power, and a high level of responsibility that accompanies it. Despite this, I am still a reasonable woman. Even the most dangerous roles require breaks, so I have bestowed them upon you as well. In your pocket, you can find a switch to transport yourself to a safe area and catch your breath. You only get three breaks however, so use them wisely.
'''“Game Paused”''' is a room similar to that within Press Start, which wanderers will be temporarily transported to when they use the switch given to them at the start of the game. It takes the appearance of a dark waiting room, complete with a table with magazines and a bottle of Almond Water. The wanderer's items will remain in The Stage where they were last left prior to pausing. This area may only be accessed up to three times throughout the game, for up to ten minutes at a time – after which the switch will disappear. One may find up to ten [[Glowfolk]] in this area, who will offer tips and tricks for beating the game; though they will severely punish those deemed to be cheaters. ==You Cheated==
Those of you who are actually reading know full well the rules in play, and you should know better than to end up here. This place is meant purely to punish those with foul behavior, and there is nothing of value within the hell I created. I have spent the entire existence of the Backrooms itself slowly developing its laws, and I do not appreciate those who spit upon my work. If you seek my help yet you disrespect me and my work to the highest degree, you have no business being anywhere near my good graces. It is true that I seek determination in my winners, but a foul rule breaker is not worthy of my prize.
{{Blurimage|align=right|caption='''''Someone is coming.'''''|width=275px|file=You Cheated.webp}} '''“You Cheated”''' is a detainment zone within The Game which those deemed "cheaters" are held prisoner. It lies "out of bounds" of The Stage, and thus is not normally accessible unless one "cheats" – typically by attempting to exploit or tamper with some aspect of The Game. The area itself is a small blank room, with an ajar doorway leading into darkness. Beyond the doorway is a more dangerous and hazardous version of the maze from The Stage. All the arcade machines will display the text: '''''Nobody likes a cheater.''''' It should be noted that, after certain periods of the wanderer's time in this area, it undergoes changes.
After five minutes, small pieces of the ceiling will collapse and some hostile entities will begin appearing.
After ten minutes, the rate of changes will increase significantly. Large portions of the ceiling will collapse (often directly above the wanderer), and the walls will also begin to collapse. This allows the hostile entities present in this area to locate the wanderer more easily.
After twenty minutes, most of the electronics will spontaneously combust and set aflame, with the resulting fires quickly engulfing most of the area.
At the thirty-minute mark, the remaining light sources within the area will explode, plunging it into complete darkness. Any fire in this area will continue to exist, but will no longer emit light, making it impossible to see. If the wanderer is still in the first room by this point, they will have one minute to cross the doorway before the room and everything in it disintegrates.
If the wanderer manages to survive for an entire hour, they will be given an arcade token, and their final task is to put it into an arcade cabinet. Doing so will transport the wanderer to the level they were previously in prior to The Game, but they will be disqualified from ever being able to play the game again – unable to re-enter the level. It is unknown what happens beyond one hour. ==Game Over==
You fought hard, played hard, but you ultimately didn’t possess the skill to win my game. Or maybe you didn’t try at all. Either way, you are here now, and likely trapped here until your inevitable death. For a select few of you who were skilled enough however, there is hope. If you happened to make a very close friend and ally throughout the game, or you found a continue throughout your travels, there may very well be an escape from my labyrinth. But for the rest of you, it is truly game over.
{{Blurimage|align=right|caption=The Game Creator in Game Over. The wanderer put no effort into their game.|width=275px|file=Game Over.png}} '''“Game Over”''' is the area that wanderers are sent to after dying three times within The Game. It consists of a series of abandoned hallways that lead to nowhere, sparsely populated with hostile entities. Rooms of a wide variety may occasionally be found in this area, ranging from abandoned bedrooms, living rooms, offices, etc. These rooms usually have supplies, though they are equally likely to house hostile entities as well. Upon entering this level, wanderers will find the Game Creator standing before them. Her demeanor will vary depending on how well the game was played. If time and effort was put into it, relative to the wanderer's skill, there will be a higher chance of encountering an exit to the level the wanderer was previously in before entering The Game. However, if the Game Creator determines that the wanderer did not put enough effort into the game, she will abandon them, leaving them in this area to find for themselves. There is, however, a third possibility. If a wanderer was able to recover an arcade token hidden somewhere in The Stage, they may present it to the Game Creator and be granted one extra life, with no progress lost in their game. They will thus respawn where they last died in The Stage. If The Game is explored with a group, Game Over will be experienced differently. Those who end up here will be kept in holding cells until/unless everyone in the group loses all 3 lives, at which point they will experience this area the same as individual wanderers. It is unknown what else happens to those within the holding cells if part of the group still remains alive. ==You Win!==
You fought hard, played hard, and made it to the end. This place is your reward for making it trough the huge majority of game. Anyone here is in my good graces at the very least, as you have proven yourself able to conquer my immensely difficult challenges.
'''“You Win!”''' is the final area of The Game, accessed only by defeating The Boss and reaching the exit of The Stage. It is a singular 11-story building, seemingly in the middle of [[The Void|nowhere]]. Upon entering this level, wanderers will find themselves in a series of fully furnished unoccupied bedrooms, similar to those in a hotel, on the top floor. Further exploration will reveal an elevator which grants access to the lower floors of this area, each vastly different from the top one. * The tenth floor is an office space, without any noteworthy features. * The ninth floor is a computer lab filled with self-repairing computer terminals, though they seem to be permanent fixtures of this area thus cannot be removed. Most information about The Game has come from those who have uploaded reports to the [[Backrooms Wiki|database]] from these computers. * The eighth floor is a movie theater, which may play a wide variety of movies from any point in time in any reality. A flat screen TV display on the left side of the room that has a list of scheduled movies, and to the right is a snack bar. Entities and wanderers may be seen watching movies on this floor. * The seventh floor is a dark lounge area where wanderers and non-hostile entities may relax, though it is advised not to make too much noise. * The sixth floor is a Glowfolk-run night club. The clubgoers are quite nice, willing to provide food and liquids to wanderers. However, if one causes too much of a disturbance, they may be defenestrated by a Red Glowfolk. * The fifth floor is a makeshift food court run by facelings, each with their own distinct restaurant. * The fourth floor is a dining area where those who order food from the fifth floor may eat it. Purple Glowfolk may be found playing card games. * The third floor consists of a supermarket that is fully functional and stocked with fresh supplies. Wanderers may also retrieve their favorite item from The Stage here. * The second floor is a gift shop run by Facelings, who will give you an item for free if you get them food from the fifth floor. * The first floor, the very bottom of the building, is the lobby. It is a common area, where people can hang out, relax, and talk. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== * Finding a poster advertising The Game. * Losing a game on an arcade machine in [[Level 399]]. * Noclipping into an electronic screen. * Finding an arcade machine on any stage of [[Level 174]]. ===Exits=== * Selecting "Decline" on the arcade machine in Press Start will bring you back to the previous level you were in. * Escaping You Cheated will bring you back to the previous level you were in, but you will be prevented from re-entering. * Being judged by the Game Creator to have played a good game in Game Over will send you back to the previous level you were in, with the ability to re-enter in the future. * Noclipping into a chair on the fifth floor of You Win will lead to the [[Level 610|Snackrooms]]. * A wooden door on the first floor of You Win with a sign that says "Goodbye" will lead to [[The Hub]]. * Play The Game Again? -----
